methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers in robotics and manufacturing (1 public, 8 private), including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Given the nascent stage and lack of sufficient narrow-set peers at seed or adjacent stages with comparable valuations, peer-median analysis is not applicable. The primary valuation method employed is stage-progression, which applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the next financing stage. Using the $7M total raised as a proxy for current valuation, this method yields an implied valuation of $17.5M.

The stage-progression method supports a valuation range of $14.9M to $20.1M, with a median point of $17.5M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable seed-stage peers in the robotics and manufacturing sector—the 11 same-sector companies identified did not meet the threshold of 3+ datapoints in narrow stage or valuation-tier buckets. This limited peer set means the analysis relies heavily on generic seed-to-next-stage step-up assumptions rather than sector-specific calibration. Additional comparable company data at the seed stage would reduce reliance on this mechanical approach.
